rules.title: Explicit role is permitted on the HTML element
rules.applicability: HTML element has an explicit role
rules.expectation: The role is allowed for that element and context.

rules.title: ARIA state or property is permitted
rules.applicability: HTML element has an aria-* attribute
rules.expectation: Each state or property is allowed for the element and computed role.

rules.title: ARIA does not conflict with strong native semantics
rules.applicability: ARIA changes or supplements native HTML semantics
rules.expectation: The exposed semantics remain consistent with the actual element behavior.

rules.title: Role-required states and properties are present
rules.applicability: An ARIA role has required states or properties
rules.expectation: All required states and properties are present and current.

rules.title: ARIA values use the required datatype and token
rules.applicability: Element has an ARIA state or property
rules.expectation: The value is valid for the attribute and reflects the current UI state.

rules.title: ARIA ID references resolve to intended targets
rules.applicability: ARIA attribute contains an ID reference or ID reference list
rules.expectation: Every required reference resolves and expresses the intended relationship.

rules.title: Accessible name is permitted, present, and appropriate
rules.applicability: Element is named through content, HTML, or ARIA
rules.expectation: Naming is permitted and the computed name identifies the visible purpose.

rules.title: Deprecated ARIA features are not introduced
rules.applicability: Element uses an explicit role or aria-* attribute
rules.expectation: No deprecated role, state, property, or attribute is introduced.

rules.title: Role, state, property, and token case is valid
rules.applicability: Element uses an explicit role or aria-* attribute
rules.expectation: Names and values follow the applicable case requirements.

rules.title: Owned descendants and required context roles are valid
rules.applicability: Role constrains owned descendants or required context
rules.expectation: The accessibility-tree hierarchy satisfies role ownership and context requirements.

rules.title: Native HTML is preferred when it provides the required behavior
rules.applicability: Custom role or ARIA reproduces a native HTML control
rules.expectation: The implementation documents why native HTML is insufficient or uses the native element.

rules.title: Redundant explicit roles are avoided
rules.applicability: Explicit role duplicates the implicit HTML role
rules.expectation: Redundant role markup is removed unless a documented compatibility need exists.
